meaning: adj.单手的,单独的,独力的,单枪匹马的
examples: It took courage to sail the Atlantic singlehanded.
The other man saw that he had no chance singlehanded against the bear .
Should I have enough money, I should sail around the world singlehanded .
His one-and-a-half revolutions of the stormy southern ocean, before at last dropping anchor in Tahiti, is one of the great feats of singlehanded sailing.
Legend has it that ancient Rome was spared a devastating invasion by the courage and skill of a great warrior named Horatius, whose singlehanded defense of a bridge kept the enemy hordes at bay.
